tasks.title: Build CDC sync Lambda
tasks.description:
CDC sync Lambda that processes Salesforce Platform Event changes and keeps Turbopuffer in sync.

Transport: Salesforce CDC Platform Events → CometD/Pub-Sub API → Lambda trigger (via EventBridge pipe or direct subscription).
Auth: Org-level Connected App (same as bulk loader, Client Credentials flow).
Replay: Use replayId to resume from last processed event on Lambda cold start. Store last replayId in DynamoDB (single row).
Batching: Process CDC events in micro-batches (up to 10 events per invocation). Each event may contain multiple field changes.
Failure: Failed events go to SQS DLQ. DLQ consumer retries with exponential backoff (3 attempts).
Denormalization: On CREATE/UPDATE, fetch full record via SOQL (not just changed fields), denormalize per config, embed, upsert. On DELETE, delete by ID.
Cascade: Deferred to v1 (parent changes do not propagate to children in POC).

Design decision needed before implementation: EventBridge pipe vs. direct Pub-Sub API subscription in Lambda. EventBridge pipe is simpler (managed) but adds latency. Direct subscription gives lower latency but requires CometD client management in Lambda.
acceptance_criteria: Processes CDC events for Property, Lease, Availability (object mapping covers all three)
On CREATE/UPDATE: fetches full record via SOQL, denormalizes per config, embeds, upserts via SearchBackend
On DELETE: deletes document from SearchBackend by record ID
Input adapter decouples transport from sync logic — AppFlow adapter ships, Pub/Sub stubbed
Failed events routed to SQS DLQ
Handles batch CDC events (multiple changes in one invocation)
Latency: design supports <5 minute sync (AppFlow polling + Lambda processing)
scripts/bulk_load.py still passes all tests after denormalize extraction

progress_notes.note: Started by Todd Terry

Code complete: lib/denormalize.py extracted, lambda/cdc_sync/index.py with input adapter pattern (AppFlow shipped, Pub/Sub stubbed), CDCEvent dataclass, DELETE handling, DLQ routing, freshness metrics. 26 tests + 31 denormalize tests pass. Design decision: AppFlow→S3→EventBridge transport. ReplayId deferred to Pub/Sub adapter.
Task completed
Merged to main: a314217

tasks.title: Regression root-cause analysis

progress_notes.note:
Validator follow-up items from 3.1 (2026-03-18 rerun, artifact: results/validate_data_20260318T055130Z.txt):

Check 2 FAIL — Object type counts: deal and sale objects have 0 docs in Turbopuffer. Config (denorm_config.yaml) defines 5 object types but only 3 were bulk-loaded (property: 603, lease: 225, availability: 158). Root cause: bulk_load was never run for ascendix__Deal__c or ascendix__Sale__c. Fix: run bulk_load for deal/sale, or remove them from denorm_config.yaml if the sandbox has no records.

Check 5 FAIL — Parent fields: (a) property: 0/10 docs have market or submarket parent keys (market_name, submarket_name) — sandbox properties may not have ascendix__Market__c/ascendix__SubMarket__c populated, or parent lookup failed during bulk_load. (b) lease: 6/10 docs have partial property keys — missing property_propertysubtype and property_totalbuildingarea because source property records have null values for those fields. (c) availability: 10/10 docs have partial property keys — same null-source-field issue. Fix: investigate sandbox data; if fields are genuinely null, validator should distinguish null-in-source from lookup-failure.

Check 7 WARN — Hybrid search: turbopuffer SDK 1.17.0 returns 422 'Failed to deserialize RankInput' when backend builds rank_by=('Sum', ('text','BM25',q), ('vector','ANN',v)). Validator falls back to vector-only and reports WARN. Fix: investigate SDK rank_by serialization format for hybrid queries.

Check 8 FAIL — Warm latency: p95=155ms against 50ms threshold. The 50ms target is for in-region Lambda; local cross-internet runs from Mac to GCP US-Central have ~50ms network RTT alone. Fix: either accept higher threshold for local validation or run from Lambda for definitive measurement.

Root-cause analysis complete (2026-03-18). Task 3.1 acceptance suite is 15/15 — no regression queries. Scope broadened to carried-forward validator issues.

FINDINGS (5):

F-1 (Check 2) — Deal/Sale 0 docs. Classification: missing bulk-loaded objects + scope mismatch. bulk_load.py never run for Deal/Sale. Additionally, query-layer scope (system_prompt.py:223) explicitly excludes Deal/Sale while denorm_config.yaml and README include them in POC scope. Three-way inconsistency: indexed scope (denorm_config), CDC scope (cdc_sync CDC_ENTITY_MAP), and query-time scope (system_prompt TOOL_DEFINITIONS) disagree. Fix: either narrow indexed/CDC scope to match query-time scope, or expand query-time scope to match indexed scope. Product decision required. → 3.3.2

F-2 (Check 7) — Hybrid 422 RankInput. Classification: SDK serialization bug. turbopuffer_backend.py:111 uses ('Sum', *rank_parts) but SDK 1.17.0 expects ('Sum', rank_parts) per Tuple[Literal['Sum'], Sequence[RankByText]] type signature. One-line fix. → 3.3.2

F-3 (Check 5, property) — 0/10 market/submarket parent keys. Classification: null source data. Sandbox Property records lack Market/SubMarket FK values. Pipeline correctly skips nulls (denormalize.py:138-142). → Non-blocking (sandbox data quality)

F-4 (Check 5, lease/availability) — Partial property parent keys. Classification: null source data. Parent Property records have null PropertySubType and TotalBuildingArea. Same correct-skip behavior. → Non-blocking

F-5 (Check 8) — p95=155ms vs 50ms. Classification: environment artifact. Local Mac-to-GCP RTT dominates. 50ms target is for in-region Lambda (CLAUDE.md Known Failure Pattern 3). → Non-blocking (revalidate from Lambda at go/no-go)

3.3.2 ITEMS: F-2 (hybrid rank_by one-line fix, high priority), F-1 (Deal/Sale scope alignment, pending product decision).
NON-BLOCKING: F-3, F-4 (null source data), F-5 (latency environment).

OPEN QUESTIONS: (1) Do Deal/Sale records exist in sandbox? (2) Should Deal/Sale be in POC query scope or deferred? (3) Is market/submarket data available in sandbox?

Analysis complete and committed (2731e26). All 5 carried-forward findings classified, root-caused, and scoped. Hybrid fix (F-2) implemented and validated — check 7 PASS. Deal/Sale scope (F-1) resolved as 'narrow scope now' per spec D-10. README drift fixed. CLAUDE.md updated. Non-blocking items (F-3/F-4 null source data, F-5 latency environment) documented. No remaining unresolved issues block closure.

tasks.title: Make parent-field validation sparse- and orphan-aware

progress_notes.note: Started by Todd Terry

Implementation complete (2026-03-18).

Check 5 now classifies parent-field gaps into:
- PASS: parent fields present as expected (property ownerlandlord, lease tenant/ownerlandlord)
- WARN sparse: 0/N docs have parent keys, SF confirms 0 FKs populated
- WARN stale: 0/N docs have parent keys, SF confirms FKs ARE populated → needs reindex
- WARN partial (null source): consistent absent fields across docs → null on source parent
- WARN no docs: object type not loaded in index
- FAIL inconsistent partial: different fields missing on different docs → possible defect

With SF client (--target-org): queries Salesforce to distinguish sparse vs stale.
Without SF client: reports as 'sparse or stale — source verification unavailable'.

progress_notes.note: Started by Todd Terry

tasks.description: New lambda/poll_sync/ handler. On invocation: (1) read poll-eligible object list from POLL_OBJECTS env var (comma-separated SObject API names, e.g. ascendix__Deal__c,ascendix__Sale__c); (2) for each object, read last-sync watermark from SSM param /salesforce-ai-search/poll-watermark/{object} (default to epoch if missing); (3) query Salesforce: SELECT ... FROM {object} WHERE LastModifiedDate > {watermark}; (4) for each changed record, denormalize + embed + upsert to Turbopuffer (reuse denorm/embed/upsert from bulk_load path); (5) write new watermark = max(LastModifiedDate) back to SSM; (6) emit CloudWatch metrics (records_synced per object). Support an optional event payload field 'objects' to override POLL_OBJECTS for on-demand single-object runs. Support 'full_sync: true' in event payload to ignore watermark and re-index all records for specified objects.

tasks.description: Build a config control plane around the actual Ascendix Search payload model so admin changes in Selected Objects, field filters, Default Layout rows, saved searches, relationship paths, and result columns can be detected, compiled into runtime artifacts, classified by impact, and applied without relying on ad hoc manual regeneration. Ascendix Search should be used as an admin-intent signal and structural validation reference, not as the capability ceiling of the natural language search product. See docs/architecture/ascendix_search_config_refresh_plan.md and docs/architecture/ascendix_search_signal_priority_and_validation.md.
acceptance_criteria: A versioned compiled config artifact exists per org and is derived from normalized Ascendix Search source config, including concatenated Selected Objects* rows, Default Layout rows, saved-search templates, and Salesforce metadata.
Admin config changes are detected, diffed, and classified into no-op, prompt-only, field-scope, relationship, or object-scope impacts using the normalized Ascendix Search payload model.
Safe changes can be applied without code redeploy, and reindex-heavy changes route through an explicit apply workflow.

Validation uses Ascendix Search as a structural reference for object scope, field allowlists, default columns, and relationship paths while preserving freedom for the NL search system to exceed Ascendix's query-builder patterns.
tasks.technical_context: The demo org stores Ascendix Search config in ascendix_search__SearchSetting__c.Name plus ascendix_search__Value__c, not in dedicated SelectedObjects/ResultColumns fields. Selected Objects is chunked across multiple rows and must be concatenated before JSON parsing. Default layouts are stored as Default Layout * rows whose Value__c is a JSON array of result-column expressions. Saved searches remain in ascendix_search__Search__c.Template__c and are useful as relationship and filter fixtures for structural validation. This control plane should inform indexing and denorm, not constrain NL query behavior to Ascendix Search parity.

tasks.title: Fix click-to-record navigation in LWC search results

progress_notes.note: Started by Todd Terry

Replaced NavigationMixin.Navigate with direct /lightning/r/{recordId}/view URL in navigateToRecord() and navigateToRecordWithObjectType(). Always opens in new tab. Committed and pushed. Needs SF deploy (sf project deploy) to take effect.
Task completed

Root cause was deeper than NavigationMixin: lightning-formatted-rich-text strips data-* attributes and custom classes from <a> tags, so the href=# + click-interception approach silently fails. Fixed by replacing href=# with direct /lightning/r/{recordId}/view URLs and target=_blank. Links now survive sanitization. Deployed to sandbox.

Second fix: lightning-formatted-rich-text also strips relative URLs (no protocol). Changed from /lightning/r/{id}/view to window.location.origin + /lightning/r/{id}/view to produce absolute https:// URLs that survive sanitization. Deployed.

Verified working by user. Root cause chain: (1) NavigationMixin fails in custom Lightning App context, (2) href=# with data-* click interception fails because lightning-formatted-rich-text strips data-* and custom classes, (3) relative URLs /lightning/r/{id}/view stripped because rich-text only allows absolute http/https URLs. Final fix: window.location.origin + /lightning/r/{id}/view produces absolute URLs that survive sanitization and open records in new tab.
Task completed

tasks.description: Prevent the /query agent from presenting best-effort search samples as authoritative ranked leaderboards when the user asks for grouped top-N queries that require a clear metric and a single valid grouping dimension. This task covers queries like 'top brokers by deal size' where the current indexed schema has multiple broker role fields and the agent currently fabricates a ranking from individual search hits instead of clarifying or declining.
tasks.technical_context: Current /query architecture passes the raw user question directly into the Bedrock tool-use loop in lambda/query/index.py and lib/query_handler.py; there is no separate query-rewrite/decomposition stage on this path. The current prompt in lib/system_prompt.py also says 'Search first, clarify later', which biases the model toward acting on slightly ambiguous questions. At the backend layer, aggregate_records supports count/sum/avg plus group_by, but Turbopuffer aggregation is computed client-side in lib/turbopuffer_backend.py and only groups on a single indexed attribute. Deal records expose multiple broker role fields (tenant_rep_broker_name, listing_broker_company_name, buyer_rep_name, lead_broker_company_name) rather than one canonical broker dimension. Fix should prefer a targeted guard or lightweight preflight interpretation for ambiguous leaderboard queries over a full legacy-style query decomposer. Acceptable solutions include: (1) prompt/tooling guardrails that force clarification when the metric or grouping field is ambiguous, (2) backend/tool-dispatch support for explicit top-N grouped aggregate results if a single valid dimension exists, and/or (3) a narrow pre-LLM intent/guard layer for unsupported grouped-entity leaderboards. Do not reintroduce the old retrieve-path schema decomposer as the primary /query architecture without a strong justification.
acceptance_criteria: Queries that ask for grouped leaderboards or top-N rankings with an ambiguous metric or ambiguous grouping dimension (for example 'top brokers by deal size') no longer return fabricated rankings from individual search hits; they either clarify the metric/dimension or state the capability gap plainly.

When a leaderboard query does map to a single valid grouped aggregate in the indexed schema, the system returns results derived from aggregate_records or equivalent backend-supported grouping logic, with deterministic sorting and top-N truncation rather than ad hoc LLM ranking.

Regression coverage exists for both supported and unsupported leaderboard-style queries, including at minimum: top markets by deal count, top brokers by clearly specified metric and role (if supported), and ambiguous broker leaderboard requests that must clarify or decline.
tasks.gate_criteria: Use the current prompt/tool surface as the baseline and tighten behavior for leaderboard queries without regressing existing 11-object query support.

tasks.description: Support the class of /query requests that are not directly answerable as written but become answerable with one or two constrained clarifications. Replace unsupported freeform follow-up expectations with an explicit clarification UX so users can choose the intended metric, grouping dimension, or role without requiring full chat memory.
tasks.technical_context: Current LWC behavior is single-shot: handleSubmit resets the current answer and sends only the current textarea value plus sessionId from salesforce/lwc/ascendixAiSearch/ascendixAiSearch.js, and Apex forwards session_id in salesforce/classes/AscendixAISearchController.cls. But /query does not use session_id for conversational memory; lambda/query/index.py only uses it for namespace warm-cache and lib/query_handler.py constructs a fresh message list for each request. That means a user typing 'yes, that's what I want' has no prior clarification context on the backend. Recommended implementation path is pragmatic, not a full chat system: when the system detects an answerable-with-clarification query, return a structured clarification payload or answer footer with explicit options (for example gross deal value vs gross fee vs square footage; lead broker vs tenant rep vs listing broker). The LWC should render those options as buttons/chips or prefilled next-query actions that submit a rewritten explicit question, rather than relying on freeform conversational memory. A later enhancement can add short-lived pending interpretation state keyed by session_id if freeform clarification becomes necessary, but the first fix should avoid requiring generic multi-turn memory.

tasks.title: Agent write-back: edit, create, and multi-record workflows

progress_notes.note: Revised after QA/feasibility review. Added missing shared-foundation work for writable metadata and structured proposal transport; narrowed UI subtasks so they match current repo architecture and supported Salesforce form/navigation behavior.

tasks.description: Enable AI-assisted Salesforce write-back through standard Salesforce forms. Build this in two shared foundations first: (1) a writable field metadata/eligibility contract that separates real Salesforce write targets from denormalized search fields, and (2) a structured write-proposal transport from the query stack through Apex into LWC state. Scenario A is edit existing record via diff review plus embedded form scoped to writable fields. Scenario B is create new record via preview plus NavigationMixin native create with encoded default values. Scenario C is deferred multi-record orchestration built on A and B. No direct Lambda-side Salesforce API DML for user-initiated writes.
acceptance_criteria: Shared foundations exist before scenario UI work: writable-field metadata excludes read-only, formula, system, and denormalized fields from proposal scope, and structured write proposals reach the LWC without relying on bracketed answer markers.

Scenario A (edit): user asks to change an existing record -> agent identifies the target record and valid writable fields -> diff view shows Field Label, Current Value, and Proposed Value -> user advances into an embedded Salesforce edit form scoped to the proposed writable fields -> save runs through standard Salesforce form submission and surfaces standard validation/FLS behavior -> chat and toast show success with a record link.

Scenario B (create): user asks to create a new record -> agent proposes object type, writable field defaults, and resolved parent lookups -> preview modal shows the proposed values -> user opens the native Salesforce create experience with encoded defaultFieldValues prefilled -> remaining required fields can be completed and saved through the standard Salesforce path.

The implementation does not claim create success or cancel outcomes in chat after native NavigationMixin create unless a supported detection mechanism is implemented and validated. If no such mechanism exists, the UX must fail safe and return the user to chat without false confirmation.
Scenario C (multi-record) is deferred until Scenarios A and B are solid and uses the same metadata and transport foundations plus sequential orchestration and ID chaining.

tasks.description: When the user is on a Salesforce record page and asks the agent to edit the record, the propose_edit tool fails ~75% of the time with 'Write proposal rejected — invalid Salesforce Id'. Root cause: index.py:319-323 builds the effective question with record_name and record_type but omits record_id. The model has no reliable way to get the Salesforce ID of the viewed record, so it either passes the record name as the ID ('Todco Investments'), hallucinates an ID, or asks the user for the ID. Without record page context the model always searches first and succeeds 100%. Fix: (A) Include record_id in the effective question bracket so the model has the real ID. (B) Add Salesforce ID format validation in normalize_propose_edit_input so invalid IDs produce a clear tool error the model can self-correct from, rather than failing opaquely in Apex. (C) Add prompt guidance telling the model to use the exact id from search results or record context — never fabricate.

tasks.description:
Switch Salesforce namespace embeddings from Bedrock Titan v2 to Bedrock Cohere Embed v4 so the indexed vectors are compatible with xiq-kw query-time embedding.

## Current implementation
- lib/denormalize.py uses EMBEDDING_MODEL_ID = "amazon.titan-embed-text-v2:0" and EMBEDDING_DIMENSIONS = 1024.
- scripts/bulk_load.py sends Titan requests via _generate_embedding_request().
- lambda/cdc_sync/index.py sends Titan requests via _embed_single().
- lambda/poll_sync/index.py sends Titan requests via _embed_single() / _embed_batch().
- scripts/validate_data.py has its own local copies of EMBEDDING_MODEL_ID and EMBEDDING_DIMENSIONS plus an inline Titan embed call.

## Required embedding contract
- Model: us.cohere.embed-v4:0 (Bedrock inference profile)
- Dimensions: 1024
- Index-time mode: search_document
- Bedrock request body:
  {"texts": ["..."], "input_type": "search_document", "embedding_types": ["int8"], "truncate": "END", "output_dimension": 1024}
- Persistence boundary: cast returned int8 embedding values to list[float] before document build/upsert. This preserves float-typed vectors across build_document(), SearchBackend, and Turbopuffer while matching xiq-kw query-time int8 representation.
- xiq-kw will use the paired search_query mode at query time. This task is index-time only.

## Code paths that must be updated
- lib/denormalize.py — canonical constants (model ID changes to us.cohere.embed-v4:0)
- scripts/bulk_load.py — _generate_embedding_request() Titan request/response shape -> Cohere request/response + int8-to-float cast
- lambda/cdc_sync/index.py — _embed_single() Titan request/response shape -> Cohere request/response + int8-to-float cast
- lambda/poll_sync/index.py — _embed_single() / _embed_batch() Titan request/response shape -> Cohere request/response + int8-to-float cast
- scripts/validate_data.py — local constant copies and inline Titan embed call
- tests/test_denormalize.py — hardcoded assertion for Titan model ID
- tests/test_bulk_load.py — asserts Titan request fields (inputText, dimensions)
- tests/test_cdc_sync.py — Titan embedding mocks and assertions
- tests/test_poll_sync.py — Titan embedding mocks and assertions

## Must remain unchanged
- Namespace format: org_{salesforce_org_id} in lambda/cdc_sync/index.py
- Turbopuffer document shape from build_document() in lib/denormalize.py
- text BM25 field/schema in FULL_TEXT_SEARCH_SCHEMA
- Cleaned direct-field keys and prefixed parent-field keys
- Query Lambda / tool-dispatch behavior
- Float-typed vector interface across build_document(), SearchBackend, and Turbopuffer upsert

## Non-goals
- No /query redesign
- No field alias or schema-contract changes beyond embedding model swap
- No mixed-model compatibility layer
- No public type churn (vectors stay list[float] at persistence boundary)

## Critical note
Reindex is mandatory. Changing query-time embeddings without rebuilding the SF namespace is incompatible. No mixed Titan/Cohere vectors may remain.
acceptance_criteria: Bulk load writes Cohere Embed v4 1024-dim vectors using Bedrock search_document
CDC sync writes the same Cohere Embed v4 1024-dim vectors
Poll sync writes the same Cohere Embed v4 1024-dim vectors
Existing namespace is fully rebuilt after the switch; no mixed Titan/Cohere vectors remain
Live ANN query against the rebuilt namespace using a Cohere search_query vector returns relevant Salesforce records, and existing BM25/filter queries still work unchanged

If the broker is absent in a user-facing Sale result, the likely defect is downstream of denormalization (query shaping / card rendering / field selection), not in the current Sale audit payload.

Turbopuffer limit research as of 2026-04-16: official docs at https://turbopuffer.com/docs/limits list the following current production limits relevant to this investigation: Max attribute names per namespace = 256, Max attribute name length = 128 bytes, Max filterable value size = 4 KiB, Max attribute value size = 8 MiB, Max vector columns per namespace = 2, Max dimensions = 10,752, Max document size = 64 MiB. The immediate Sale-comp risk in this repo is the 256-attribute namespace cap, not value size. Repo evidence already records live contact with this limit: tasks.json notes under earlier work say the namespace attribute count exceeded 256 after relationship metadata expansion and the indexed relationship payload had to be trimmed. docs/architecture/object_scope_and_sync.md also states Turbopuffer has a hard 256-attribute namespace cap. Operational implication for task 4.25: adding Property Street, PostalCode, YearBuilt, plus any new sale-side broker attribute must be evaluated against the shared namespace-wide 256 attribute-name ceiling. Because the cap is per namespace, not per object, even a surgical Sale config addition can require trimming low-value existing attributes elsewhere in the Sale shape to stay within the limit.

Replay guardrails (explicit, supersedes AC5 where they differ): (a) only after compiler fix is deployed AND scripts/run_config_refresh.py --apply has advanced the active-version SSM pointer to a verified-clean artifact; (b) re-seed watermarks for Account, Contact, and ascendix__Property__c to '2026-04-16T06:45:00Z|' via aws ssm put-parameter --type String (NOT SecureString — lambda/poll_sync/index.py:237 reads without WithDecryption=True; a SecureString seed would silently fall back to epoch at :241 and re-poll all history); (c) invoke salesforce-ai-search-poll-sync one object at a time with payload {"objects":["<single>"],"full_sync":false} — never batch all three in a single invocation (5-min Lambda timeout, sequential processing, any failure aborts remaining objects); (d) persist each raw Lambda response to /tmp/poll_<Object>_iter<N>.json; (e) loop per-object only while that object's response reports continuation_needed:true; (f) verification for each of the 3 objects requires all four: raw response (statusCode=200, FunctionError=None, summary.<Object>.records_synced, summary.<Object>.watermark, summary.<Object>.continuation_needed), poll_sync CloudWatch logs showing 'Polling <Object> from watermark:' through 'Poll sync for <Object> complete:' with no ERROR lines, SalesforceAISearch/PollSync.RecordsSynced metric emitted with SObject dimension, AND user-visible /query validation for at least one record per object known to have been modified during 2026-04-16 → recovery (if such a record exists — if records_synced=0, note this in the task as a proven-empty gap, not as missing validation).

tasks.description: Operational recovery work for the 2026-04-16 to 2026-04-21 CDC outage. Two compounding failures: (1) all 5 AppFlow Salesforce CDC flows (Account, Contact, Property, Lease, Availability) were Suspended with Cometd replay IDs rejected because Salesforce aged out the saved replay position during idle time; (2) the /salesforce/access_token SSM parameter was 5 days stale, causing cdc_sync to fail with SalesforceAuthenticationError on any enrichment path. Recovery strategy: rotate SSM token, merge-preserving Lambda env bump to force cold start on cdc_sync and poll_sync, add appflowGeneration CDK context param so renaming flowName forces CloudFormation to replace (delete+create) the 5 flows with fresh Cometd subscriptions, redeploy Ingestion stack, then watermarked backfill via poll_sync one-object-at-a-time with String watermarks seeded at 2026-04-16T06:45:00Z. Phase A (live restore) completed and verified end-to-end. Phase B (backfill) partial: 2 of 5 objects proven gap-free (ascendix__Lease__c and ascendix__Availability__c both returned records_synced=0 because no records were modified during the outage window), 3 of 5 objects (Account, Contact, ascendix__Property__c) blocked by a pre-existing compiler bug surfaced during today's work and deferred to task 4.26. Live CDC is fully restored; the deferred objects do not have known-missed data but cannot be confirmed gap-free until 4.26 ships and backfill replays.
acceptance_criteria: Phase A (live restore) complete: 5 AppFlow flows with -v2-20260421 suffix Active (renamed via CDK appflowGeneration context, replacement forced by flowName change — only Replacement:True property on CfnFlow). cdc_sync logs show Upserted success and zero INVALID_SESSION_ID after SSM token rotation. End-to-end /query validation confirmed the pipeline delivers a Salesforce edit to Turbopuffer within ~15 seconds (total_lag metric).
Phase B (backfill) partial: ascendix__Lease__c records_synced=0, ascendix__Availability__c records_synced=0, both with SOQL returning 'No more records' — proven gap-free, not silent failures.

Phase B blocked for Account, Contact, and ascendix__Property__c: active compiled runtime config artifact (compiled/20260415T192655-75e7d0ebea94.yaml) contains bare relationship names in Account/Contact embed_fields (Owner, Account) and cross-object field contamination plus self-reference in ascendix__Property__c embed_fields (13 non-Property fields from Deal/Sale/Availability/Lease). Salesforce rejects the generated SOQL with INVALID_FIELD. cdc_sync is unaffected because it consumes the CDC payload; only poll_sync triggers the bug. Deferred to 4.26.

Memory-worthy takeaways saved to auto-memory: feedback_lambda_env_merge (merge-preserve env vars), feedback_cf_resource_replacement (force CF replacement via Replacement:True property + change-set confirmation), feedback_ssm_parameter_type_match (SSM Type must match reader's decryption expectations), feedback_user_visible_validation (logs are not user-visible validation).

This task cannot be marked complete until 4.26 lands and Account/Contact/Property backfill replay closes the outage gap for the 3 deferred objects. Per CLAUDE.md rule 6, the outstanding live-validation on 3/5 objects is an explicit blocker; keep status in_progress.

tasks.title: Unify cdc_sync runtime config loading with query and poll_sync
tasks.description: cdc_sync currently loads only the bundled denorm_config.yaml, while query and poll_sync load the active compiled artifact from S3 via CONFIG_ARTIFACT_BUCKET and the active-version pointer. This creates runtime config divergence: poll_sync can fail on compiled-artifact bugs that cdc_sync does not see, and CDC freshness behavior may not match query/runtime scope. Keep this out of task 4.26 so the compiler fix and outage replay stay tightly scoped; implement as a separate live-CDC change with focused tests and rollback.
acceptance_criteria: cdc_sync uses RuntimeConfigLoader with the same resolution order as query/poll_sync: active S3 compiled artifact via SSM active-version pointer, then /tmp last-known-good cache, then bundled denorm_config.yaml fallback.

IngestionStack sets CONFIG_ARTIFACT_BUCKET and CONFIG_ARTIFACT_PREFIX for salesforce-ai-search-cdc-sync, grants read access to config artifacts, and grants SSM read access for /salesforce-ai-search/config/* without removing existing CDC env vars or permissions.
Unit tests cover active artifact load, last-known-good or bundled fallback behavior, and unchanged behavior when no CONFIG_ARTIFACT_BUCKET is configured.

Live CDC validation proves a controlled update for at least Property plus one standard object (Account or Contact) still enriches via SOQL and upserts successfully after cdc_sync reads runtime config from S3.
Rollback path is documented: how to revert the active-version pointer or redeploy cdc_sync without CONFIG_ARTIFACT_BUCKET, and what validation to run after rollback.

tasks.title: Optimize bulk load throughput
tasks.description: Investigate batch size tuning, embedding concurrency, and Turbopuffer upsert parallelism for large initial seeds. Currently: embed batch 25, upsert batch 100, concurrency 4. Deferred from 4.6.2.

tasks.title: Apollo.io phone number enrichment via webhook
tasks.description: Apollo phone number reveal requires reveal_phone_number=true and a webhook_url for async delivery (phones are not returned inline). Each phone reveal costs ~8 credits. Implementation: add a webhook endpoint (new Lambda or route on existing API), store phone data on receipt, and feed into the propose_edit flow. Deferred from 4.19 because: (a) async webhook adds infrastructure complexity, (b) 8 credits per phone burns through free tier instantly, (c) core enrichment value (title, email, company data) is validated without phone.

tasks.title: Express sync: immediate reindex after user-initiated record save
tasks.description: Add an express-sync path so that records saved through the AI search LWC are reindexed immediately rather than waiting for CDC or poll sync. After a successful write-back save (edit or create), the LWC calls a lightweight /express-sync endpoint that triggers single-record denormalize → embed → upsert, bypassing the AppFlow CDC pipeline entirely. This closes the UX gap where a user edits a record and then searches for it before CDC has propagated. Scope is limited to saves made through the AI search component — standard Salesforce UI edits still flow through CDC or poll sync normally. Must deduplicate against the CDC event that will also arrive for the same record. Option B from BA analysis: LWC-driven, no Apex triggers required.
acceptance_criteria: After a user saves a record through the AI search LWC (edit or create), the updated record is searchable in Turbopuffer within 3 seconds, without waiting for CDC or poll sync.
The express-sync endpoint reuses existing denormalize, embed, and upsert logic from cdc_sync — no parallel implementation of the indexing pipeline.
When the normal CDC event arrives for a record that was already express-synced, the pipeline handles it as an idempotent no-op or benign overwrite — no duplicates, no errors.
No Apex triggers are required. The express-sync call originates from the LWC after a successful DML confirmation, using the existing Named Credential.
Express sync is scoped to saves made through the AI search component only. Standard Salesforce UI edits continue through CDC or poll sync.

tasks.title: Salesforce token refresh helper: complete JWT Bearer flow to produce access_token and write to SSM
tasks.description: Today's (2026-04-21) CDC outage traced in part to /salesforce/access_token being 5 days stale in SSM — cdc_sync was failing with SalesforceAuthenticationError on enrichment describe() calls. The existing scripts/mint_jwt.py only produces the signed JWT assertion half of the JWT Bearer flow; it does NOT POST the assertion to /services/oauth2/token to exchange for an access_token. The gap is filled manually today with 'sf org display → aws ssm put-parameter', which is the same pattern referenced in archived handoffs (docs/archive/handoffs/HANDOFF-2025-12-06-DERIVED-VIEW-BACKFILL.md:19-24). This task adds scripts/refresh_sf_token.py that (1) invokes mint_jwt.py to produce a fresh signed assertion, (2) POSTs to https://test.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/token with grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer, (3) extracts access_token from the response, (4) writes to SSM /salesforce/access_token as SecureString. Turns manual multi-step rotation into one-command ops. Prerequisite for 99.6 (scheduled refresh).
acceptance_criteria: scripts/refresh_sf_token.py exists, produces a fresh Salesforce access_token via JWT Bearer flow (assertion mint + token exchange POST), and writes it to SSM /salesforce/access_token as SecureString with --overwrite.
Script is idempotent and safe to run on demand; fails loudly (non-zero exit + explicit stderr) on JWT signing error, token exchange HTTP error, or SSM write error — no partial/silent success.

Script triggers cold start of salesforce-ai-search-cdc-sync and salesforce-ai-search-poll-sync using the merge-preserving env-bump pattern documented in memory/feedback_lambda_env_merge.md (fetch current env via get-function-configuration, add a refresh key, push back as file:// JSON — never clobber the whole Variables map).

End-to-end verification: after running the script, (a) aws ssm get-parameter LastModifiedDate on /salesforce/access_token shows within the last minute, (b) a controlled SF edit flows through CDC and cdc_sync logs Upserted with zero INVALID_SESSION_ID.

tasks.title: Scheduled Salesforce token refresh: EventBridge + Lambda to run 99.5 automatically
tasks.description: Even with the one-command helper from 99.5, the rotation is still manual — which is what left /salesforce/access_token 5 days stale during the 2026-04-16 → 2026-04-21 CDC outage. This task wraps the 99.5 refresh logic in a Lambda invoked by an EventBridge rule (cron) every ~90 minutes (well inside the Salesforce access_token expiry window). Eliminates the 'SSM token is N days old' failure class entirely without requiring a full SalesforceClient refactor (which is the longer-term fix in 99.8). Lambda implementation should be a thin wrapper that calls the same JWT assertion + token exchange path as 99.5 with the private key material loaded from the SalesforceConnectedAppSecret (Secrets Manager). Monitored via CloudWatch alarm on Lambda errors.
acceptance_criteria: New Lambda function (e.g. salesforce-ai-search-sf-token-refresh) deployed via IngestionStack or a dedicated operational stack. Private key and consumer key sourced from salesforce-ai-search/appflow-creds secret and/or a dedicated private-key secret — no key material in code or env.
EventBridge rule invokes the Lambda every ~90 min (well inside Salesforce access_token expiry). Rule + Lambda provisioned in CDK, not created manually in the console.
Successful refresh updates /salesforce/access_token SSM param and bumps cdc_sync + poll_sync via merge-preserving env pattern so warm containers recycle on next invocation.
CloudWatch alarm on Lambda Errors > 0 → SNS topic → operator channel. Refresh failures must page someone — silent failure is the thing we are trying to eliminate.

Verification: kill the existing SSM token (write a known-invalid string), wait for the next scheduled run, confirm the token is refreshed automatically, cdc_sync resumes without manual intervention, and the CDC pipeline delivers a controlled SF edit end-to-end.

tasks.title: AppFlow suspension / failure alarm: detect silent multi-day CDC outage
tasks.description: During 2026-04-16 → 2026-04-21, all 5 Salesforce CDC flows were Suspended in AWS (Cometd subscription rejected by Salesforce with an aged-out replay ID) and nobody noticed for 5 days — there was no alarm. The user discovered it during an ad-hoc health check, not via proactive monitoring. This task adds a CloudWatch alarm that fires when any AppFlow Salesforce CDC flow transitions to a non-Active state OR experiences repeated subscription failures. Two viable implementations: (a) CloudWatch alarm on AWS/AppFlow FlowExecutionsFailed metric with a threshold + SNS, or (b) a lightweight Lambda scheduled every 5-10 minutes that calls appflow:ListFlows, checks flowStatus != Active for the 5 CDC flows, and publishes a custom CloudWatch metric + alarm. Option (b) is more reliable because Suspended flows emit no FlowExecutions at all — the absence of metric is exactly what we need to detect. Either way, alarm fires → SNS → operator channel. This class of failure must never be discovered by a curious user 5 days later.
acceptance_criteria: CloudWatch alarm + SNS topic provisioned in CDK (MonitoringStack or IngestionStack) that fires when any of the 5 CDC flows (Account, Contact, ascendix__Property__c, ascendix__Lease__c, ascendix__Availability__c) enters a non-Active state. Covers both 'flow Suspended by Salesforce replay error' and 'flow execution repeatedly failing'.

Implementation handles the silence-is-not-success problem: Suspended flows do NOT emit FlowExecutionsFailed metrics because no execution is attempted. Either a scheduled health-check Lambda or a metric-absence alarm pattern must be used.
Alarm latency: the 2026-04-16 → 2026-04-21 outage lasted ~5 days before discovery. This alarm must page within 15 minutes of a Suspended flow being detected.
Alarm is tested by manually stopping one CDC flow (aws appflow stop-flow), confirming the alarm fires and the SNS subscriber receives notification within the target latency, then restarting the flow.

Runbook note added to docs/runbooks/ (or environments.md) explaining recovery procedure: resume-vs-replace decision tree for Suspended flows (per 2026-04-21 incident — resume will re-fail on aged-out replay IDs; replacement via CDK appflowGeneration context is the known-good recovery).

tasks.title: SalesforceClient migration to OAuth Client Credentials with in-process refresh-on-401
tasks.description: Durable fix for the Salesforce-auth-staleness failure class that caused half of the 2026-04-21 CDC outage. Currently lambda/common/salesforce_client.py loads a static access_token from SSM (from_ssm at :136) and caches it as a module-level singleton (_salesforce_client at :494). There is no refresh path — if the token expires mid-execution, the Lambda fails with INVALID_SESSION_ID and the token stays stale in SSM until somebody runs 'sf org display → aws ssm put-parameter' manually. Tasks 99.5 and 99.6 mitigate via external rotation but add operational surface area. The durable fix is to switch SalesforceClient to OAuth Client Credentials (or JWT Bearer with in-process exchange) and catch 401 responses to refresh the token automatically in-band. This makes 99.5 and 99.6 obsolete — the token is managed inside the Lambda's auth client and SSM is no longer the source of truth. Referenced as 'Task 46' in docs/archive/handoffs/HANDOFF-2025-12-15-SCHEMA-CACHE-RECOVERY.md:139 and HANDOFF-2025-12-14-TASK35-SCHEMA-EXPORT-VERIFIED.md:178. Scope is larger than 99.5/99.6 (touches production code, needs test coverage for refresh-on-401, needs backward-compat during rollout) so explicitly sized as a Phase 99 backlog item rather than a quick-win.
acceptance_criteria: lambda/common/salesforce_client.py replaces the static-SSM access_token path with an OAuth Client Credentials (or JWT Bearer with in-process exchange) flow. Client holds credentials (consumer key + private key or client_secret) sourced from Secrets Manager, not SSM.

On any 401 / INVALID_SESSION_ID response, the client automatically refreshes the token and retries the failed request once. Refresh is rate-limited (e.g. at most once per N seconds) to avoid thundering-herd refresh storms on an auth outage.
Tests: unit tests for refresh-on-401 including retry behavior, refresh storm suppression, and graceful handling of genuine auth config errors (wrong client_id → do NOT infinite-retry).

Rollout plan in the task description: migrate cdc_sync, poll_sync, schema_discovery, schema_drift_checker one Lambda at a time, each with staged verification, before retiring the manual rotation path. Note that schema_discovery already has its own OAuth Client Credentials path at lambda/schema_discovery/discoverer.py:132 — study it as the reference implementation.

Once all Lambdas are migrated: retire /salesforce/access_token SSM param, delete scripts/refresh_sf_token.py (99.5), delete the scheduled refresh Lambda (99.6). Update memory/feedback_lambda_env_merge.md and the CDC runbook to reflect the new auth model.

tasks.title: Fix ContentNote config parent mismatch: LatestContentId -> ContentBody lacks Name field
tasks.description: Pre-existing defect surfaced during 4.26 R2 QA gate (2026-04-21). The compiled runtime config for ContentNote includes parents[LatestContentId]=[Name], but the FK LatestContentId points to ContentBody, which does not have a Name column per Salesforce describe. This produces a malformed SOQL path (SELECT LatestContent.Name FROM ContentNote) that would fail if poll_sync ever targeted ContentNote. Violation is present in both the pre-4.26 active artifact (20260415T192655-75e7d0ebea94) and the 4.26 candidate artifact (20260421T172143-75e7d0ebea94) — 4.26 does not introduce it and does not fix it. Out of scope for 4.26 because ContentNote is not in POLL_OBJECTS, is not in the CDC flow list, and is not part of the 2026-04-16 outage replay path. Fix approach: either remove LatestContentId from ContentNote parent config (if no runtime uses it), or replace the parent_field list with a real ContentBody column (e.g. FileType, or ContentDocumentId leading elsewhere). Verify via the describe-backed artifact gate script (/tmp/describe_gate.py used in 4.26 QA); after fix, the gate should report zero violations across all 13 objects. Referenced as the sole waiver on the 4.26 R2 package per QA sign-off.
acceptance_criteria: Compiler emits ContentNote.parents without the invalid LatestContentId -> [Name] entry; either the ref_field is dropped (preferred if no runtime consumer uses ContentBody context) or the parent_field list is replaced with fields that actually exist on ContentBody per describe (e.g. FileType).
Describe-backed artifact gate (the /tmp/describe_gate.py validator from the 4.26 R2 QA package) reports zero violations on the newly compiled artifact across all 13 objects.

Document in the fix PR whether runtime code actually reads parents[LatestContentId] for ContentNote anywhere — if yes, the fix must preserve equivalent behavior; if no, dropping the parent is the cleaner fix.
